ActionItems: Buy walnut and maple in bulk before the December craft fair
Raise the large cutting-board price to $45 to cover rising lumber costs
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin on Petersen Woodworks before the December St. Michael craft fair, with lumber prices creeping up.

CurrentState.Note: Garage workshop is owned outright - no rent or utilities charged to the side business.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is ~15-16 pieces a month; at ~22 pieces there is comfortable headroom. The December craft fair is the strongest single month.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Iverson)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Iverson said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.
